div1.entryFree: Indue or Endue, to endow, to supply, to furnish: Mercury i. thee with leasing, Tw. I, 5, 105. more strong reasons I shall i. you with, John IV, 2, 43. the tribunes i. you with the people's voice, Cor. II, 3, 147. Partic. --d == endowed, gifted: men --d with worthy qualities, Gent. V, 4, 153. --d with intellectual sense, Err. II, 1, 22. he is best --d in the small, LLL V, 2, 646. the full-fraught man and best --d, H5 II, 2, 139. Having to after it, to denote aim and destination: like a creature native and --d unto that element, Hml. IV, 7, 180 (supplied with qualities for that element, suited to live in water). let our finger ache, and it --s our other healthful members even to that sense of pain, Oth. III, 4, 146 (it imparts to the other limbs the faculty of feeling the same pain).
